topic.what: Lifecycle security architecture for autonomous AI agents with five coordinated protection layers spanning initialization to execution
topic.how: Defense-in-depth framework combining heterogeneous controls (static analysis, semantic checks, LLM-judge, pattern matching, least-privilege enforcement) with cross-layer risk accumulation and shared security state
topic.why_matters: Addresses cross-stage attack propagation where threats enter at one stage (e.g., input), persist through memory, and manifest only at execution—exceeding pointwise defenses like input filtering alone

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of lifecycle stages covered by dedicated protection
characteristics.evidence: Table I documents 5 layers: Foundation Scan (initialization), Input Sanitization (input), Cognition Protection (memory), Decision Alignment (decision), Execution Control (execution)
characteristics.context: Five-layer architecture aligned with agent lifecycle stages

characteristics.what_it_measures: Cross-layer risk accumulation and shared state capabilities
characteristics.value: shared security state + attack-path knowledge base
characteristics.evidence: Section III-C describes session-level risk accumulation, history-aware defense adaptation, and reusable attack-path knowledge
characteristics.context: Enables weak signals to accumulate across stages; allows prior attack patterns to inform future assessments

characteristics.evidence: Section III-A enumerates: full-lifecycle coverage, zero-trust enforcement, heterogeneous defense, cross-layer coordination
characteristics.context: Zero-trust posture assumes upstream components may be compromised; heterogeneous defense uses different mechanisms across layers to avoid correlated failures

characteristics.what_it_measures: Diversity of defensive mechanisms across layers
characteristics.evidence: Foundation Scan uses dependency analysis + supply-chain verification; Input Sanitization uses semantic + fragmented + multimodal analysis; Decision uses LLM-judge; Execution uses least-privilege + eBPF monitoring + pattern matching
characteristics.context: Each layer grounded in different defensive principles, protecting different security objects

characteristics.what_it_measures: Types of cross-stage attack chains addressed
characteristics.evidence: Case 1: malicious skill → unauthorized credential access; Case 2: indirect prompt injection → persistent memory backdoor → DoS
characteristics.context: Demonstrates lifecycle defense necessity where initialization risks manifest only at execution, and input threats persist into future sessions via memory poisoning

characteristics.what_it_measures: Scope of prototype implementation on OpenClaw
characteristics.evidence: Section IV-A: plugin-native prototype covering malicious-skill checks, tool-result contamination, memory anomaly detection, LLM-judge response review, pattern-based command interception
characteristics.context: Authors note scope sufficient for case studies but not exhaustive benchmark coverage

novelty: Cross-layer risk accumulation enabling weak-signal detection
Attack-path knowledge base for history-aware defense adaptation
Case studies demonstrating cross-stage threat propagation patterns
open_problems: Prototype coverage insufficient for exhaustive benchmarks
No quantitative performance evaluation of detection rates or false positives
Layer-specific mechanism details left unspecified (e.g., exact LLM-judge prompts)
Scalability of cross-layer coordination for complex multi-agent scenarios
Integration with diverse agent frameworks beyond OpenClaw
